items.definition_en: A supermarket is a big store where you can buy food and other things.
items.example_en: I go to the supermarket every Saturday.

items.definition_en: A mall is a large building with many shops inside.
items.example_en: We spent the afternoon at the mall.

items.definition_en: A bakery is a shop where you can buy bread and cakes.
items.example_en: She bought a fresh loaf at the bakery.

items.definition_en: A pharmacy is a place where you can buy medicine.
items.example_en: I need to go to the pharmacy for some pills.

items.definition_en: A market is a place where many people sell food and goods.
items.example_en: He buys vegetables at the market.

items.definition_en: A bookstore is a shop where you can buy books.
items.example_en: She visits the bookstore every week.

items.definition_en: A butcher is a shop that sells meat.
items.example_en: He got some beef at the butcher.

items.definition_en: A pet shop is a store where you buy animals and pet food.
items.example_en: We bought our cat from the pet shop.

items.definition_en: A flower shop is a place where you can buy flowers.
items.example_en: He stops at the flower shop to buy roses.

items.definition_en: A boutique is a small shop that sells fashionable clothes or gifts.
items.example_en: She found a nice dress at the boutique.

items.definition_en: A greengrocer is a shop that sells fruit and vegetables.
items.example_en: He buys apples from the greengrocer.

items.definition_en: A jewelry store is a shop that sells rings, necklaces, and other jewelry.
items.example_en: They bought wedding rings at the jewelry store.

items.definition_en: A toy store is a shop where you can buy toys.
items.example_en: He went to the toy store for a birthday gift.

items.definition_en: A stationery shop is a place where you buy pens, paper, and school things.
items.example_en: She bought notebooks at the stationery shop.

items.definition_en: A shoe shop is a shop where you can buy shoes.
items.example_en: He tried on new boots at the shoe shop.

items.definition_en: A clothing store is a place where you can buy clothes.
items.example_en: She prefers shopping at her local clothing store.

items.definition_en: A convenience store is a small shop open long hours that sells everyday things.
items.example_en: He grabbed some milk at the convenience store.

items.definition_en: A fishmonger is a shop that sells fish and seafood.
items.example_en: She likes to buy fresh salmon from the fishmonger.

items.definition_en: A discount store is a shop where things are sold at lower prices.
items.example_en: He got new headphones at the discount store.

items.definition_en: A hardware store is a place to buy tools, paint, and things for the home.
items.example_en: They went to the hardware store to get nails.
